name: Functional interface for an int-to-int lambda
question_text: Which functional interface from java.util.function most directly matches a lambda that takes a single int and returns an int, avoiding boxing?
general_feedback: IntUnaryOperator is specialised for int → int without boxing. Function<Integer, Integer> works but forces boxing on both sides; UnaryOperator<Integer> likewise.
correct_feedback: Correct — Primitive specialisation: int input, int output, no boxing.

answers.feedback: Primitive specialisation: int input, int output, no boxing.
It works but boxes both arguments and results; IntUnaryOperator is the primitive specialisation.
Supplier takes no input, so it cannot model a function of one argument.
Consumer returns void; it cannot produce a result.
